_comment: Workflow templates. actor: ai = Claude does it · you = manual/green-light/fix · both = collaborative. Cyber Metric computed client-side. Each step has detail (what/why-manual/how-automated) + improve (how to push it, balancing automation, quality, throughput).
philosophy: Goal isn't max automation — it's max quality output at volume, keeping you in the loop only where judgment changes the outcome.

workflows.name: DA ↔ CL — Design build
workflows.purpose: Turn an idea or spec into production-ready Figma screens.
workflows.when: Building or updating app/web screens for any brand.

steps.what: Generate the screen as JSX + design tokens
steps.how: DA designs from the spec + the brand's CLAUDE.md context
steps.detail: The Design Agent produces a working screen as code (JSX) plus design tokens, using the brand's CLAUDE.md so it starts on-brand. Automated because generating structured, on-brand layouts from a spec is exactly what a strong model does well at volume.
steps.improve: Feed DA a tighter spec template and the brand's component list up front — fewer rounds, higher first-pass quality. Quality lever: the better the brand context, the less you fix later.

steps.how: Approve or redirect BEFORE any build — the cheap gate that prevents rework
steps.detail: Manual ON PURPOSE. This is a taste/judgment call — does the direction match intent and brand feel? A model can't reliably own this, and catching a wrong direction here costs seconds vs. hours after a full build.
steps.improve: Keep it manual, but make it faster: have DA present 2–3 thumbnails side-by-side so you decide in one glance instead of reading code. This is a quality gate — don't automate it away, speed it up.

steps.how: Deterministic handoff package the builder consumes
steps.detail: A deterministic packaging step — DA writes a structured bundle that the builder reads. Fully automated because it's mechanical (no judgment), and a strict contract means errors are caught, not guessed around.
steps.improve: Already near-perfect. Add a one-line bundle summary in plain language so you can sanity-check what's being handed off without reading the file.

steps.what: Build native Figma frames with real auto-layout
steps.how: CL reads the bundle and builds via the Figma MCP
steps.detail: The builder turns the bundle into real Figma frames using its validated kit of components. Automated — and cheap, because the hard design decisions were already made; this is execution. It only rebuilds screens that changed.
steps.improve: Route this to Sonnet (not Opus) — it's execution, not reasoning. Throughput lever: cheaper + faster per screen means you can build far more screens for the same cost.

steps.how: Visual check; spacing/token corrections — your main manual cost
steps.detail: Manual today: you eyeball the built frames and fix small things (spacing, a wrong token). It's manual because pixel-level 'does this feel right' is still a human call — but a lot of it is mechanical and could be pre-checked.
steps.improve: Add an AI self-QA step before yours: have the builder list changed frames + flag any off-token values automatically. That shrinks your review to real taste calls — protects quality while cutting your time.

steps.how: You flag, AI fixes, repeat until clean
steps.detail: Collaborative loop: you point at what's off, AI fixes it. Shared because you bring the judgment of what's wrong, AI brings the speed of fixing it.
steps.improve: Batch your flags into one list and let AI fix them all in a single pass (per the prompt-batch SOP) instead of one-by-one — more throughput, same quality.

workflows.insight: Two human gates: direction (high value — kills rework early) and QA (your biggest manual cost). Keep the direction gate; shrink the QA gate with AI pre-checks.
suggestions: Add an AI self-QA step (list changed frames + flag off-token values) before your QA.
Route the build step to Sonnet, not Opus — execution, not reasoning.
Template the spec intake so DA starts with less back-and-forth.

workflows.name: Magnific — Marketing asset
workflows.when: Posters, media kits, store previews, campaign assets.

steps.how: You set intent; AI fills gaps from brand context
steps.detail: You provide the intent (what, where it runs, the message); AI fills in specs from brand context. Shared because intent is yours, but the boilerplate (dimensions, safe areas, brand palette) is automatable.
steps.improve: Save per-channel spec presets (IG story, Play Store, billboard) so the brief becomes one line. Throughput lever: faster briefs = more assets shipped.

steps.how: AI generates via Magnific — or pulls from its non-AI stock library when a real photo fits
steps.detail: AI creates imagery with Magnific, or pulls a real photo from its stock library when that's better. Automated because generating/searching visual options at volume is a model+tool strength.
steps.improve: Generate a small grid of options at once, not one image — gives you more to pick from per cycle (quantity) without more of your time.

steps.detail: AI arranges the imagery + copy into finished, on-brand layouts. Automated using your brand tokens, so variants stay consistent.
steps.improve: Once a layout wins, save it as a template so future assets skip straight to filled-in variants — compounding quality and speed.

steps.how: Pick a direction or request tweaks
steps.detail: Manual on purpose: which option is actually good is taste. AI can rank, but the final creative call is yours.
steps.improve: Keep it manual — but have AI pre-rank the variants with a one-line rationale each, so you choose faster. A quality gate worth keeping.

steps.detail: Today it's manual relaying. With the Telegram bot, AI sends to the right lead after your green-light and collects their response. Shared because you stay the gate; the routing is automated.
steps.improve: Build the Telegram bot — turns this from manual relaying into automated routing with you as the one-tap gate.

steps.detail: AI applies the lead's notes and produces final files. Automated — it's execution against clear feedback.
steps.improve: Have AI export all final sizes/formats in one go so nothing is left manual at the finish line.

workflows.insight: Your newest workflow. Highly automated except the creative green-light. The Telegram bot is the single biggest lever to raise its Cyber Metric.
suggestions: Save winning layouts as reusable templates.
Pre-define asset specs per channel so the brief is one line.
Ship the Telegram bot to automate the lead loop.

workflows.name: UX Audit
workflows.purpose: Evaluate a product and produce prioritized, actionable fixes.
workflows.when: Recurring product reviews (e.g. Tino UX Audit).

steps.detail: AI collects the current screens, flows, and the design system to review against. Automated — it's data gathering.
steps.improve: Point it at the live Figma file once and let it pull automatically each cycle.

steps.detail: AI runs usability heuristics + an accessibility check and flags issues with severity. Automated because it's pattern-matching against known principles — a model strength done at volume.
steps.improve: Feed it your own past audit notes so it learns your standards — quality compounds over cycles.

steps.what: Cluster & rank issues by user impact
steps.detail: AI groups raw issues into themes and ranks by likely user impact, so you see signal not noise. Automated synthesis.
steps.improve: Have it tie each theme to a metric or user goal so ranking reflects business value, not just count.

steps.how: Your only real input — confirm or reorder
steps.detail: Manual: you confirm or reorder the priority list. Manual because you know the business context (what's strategic this quarter) that the model doesn't.
steps.improve: Keep it — but give AI your current priorities/OKRs as context so its first draft already matches, leaving you a quick confirm.

steps.how: AI files issues straight into your task board
steps.detail: AI writes each agreed fix into Linear/Notion as a task, so the audit turns into action automatically.
steps.improve: Auto-tag project + heat so new issues appear correctly ranked on your Mission Control board.

workflows.insight: Nearly autonomous — your one input is validating priorities. Strong candidate to put on a recurring schedule so quality reviews happen at volume without your time.
suggestions: Schedule a recurring audit per active product.
Auto-file resulting issues into the task board with heat scores.

newSuggestions.title: Marketing → lead approval loop
newSuggestions.idea: Wire the Magnific workflow into the Telegram bot so lead approvals + iterations run without you relaying.

newSuggestions.title: Audit → task board pipeline
newSuggestions.basis: Tino UX Audit is a hot recurring task
newSuggestions.idea: Make UX Audit a scheduled workflow that auto-creates heat-scored issues each cycle.

newSuggestions.title: Opus → Sonnet rebalancer
newSuggestions.idea: A pre-flight checklist that downshifts routine build/QA steps to Sonnet, raising every workflow's efficiency without losing quality.
